title: K7850: BIG-IP GTM and Link Controller compatibility with AAAA and A6 record queries

metadata.concepts: A6 record ; IPv6 AAAA ; BIG-IP ; Link Controller ; BIND configuration ; NXDOMAIN response ; IP ; requesting ; virtual servers ; negative caching ; users of the affected
metadata.f5_title: BIG-IP GTM and Link Controller compatibility with AAAA and A6 record queries
metadata.excerpt: When the BIG-IP GTM or BIG-IP Link Controller systems receive an IPv6 AAAA or A6 record query for a wide IP name, and the wide IP pool has an IPv6 virtual server that is configured and available, t...

content.sfdetails__c:
Issue

Beginning in BIG-IP GTM and Link Controller 9.2.2, the gtmd process supports IPv6 AAAA and A6 record queries.

Note: 3-DNS does not support IPv6 AAAA or A6 record queries. For more information, refer to K2345: 3-DNS compatibility with AAAA record queries.

IPv6 AAAA or A6 queries processed by the BIG-IP GTM or Link Controller gtmd process

When the BIG-IP GTM or BIG-IP Link Controller systems receive an IPv6 AAAA or A6 record query for a wide IP name, and the wide IP pool has an IPv6 virtual server that is configured and available, the gtmd daemon processes the request and responds with a load-balanced response of the appropriate record type.

IPv6 AAAA or A6 queries processed by BIND

Note: The BIG-IP GTM system uses the ZoneRunner utility to manage the BIND configuration. The BIND configuration is automatically configured when a wide IP is created or modified.

Note: The BIG-IP Link Controller system does not use ZoneRunner; any BIND configuration must be performed manually.

When the BIG-IP GTM system receives an IPv6 AAAA or A6 record query for a non-wide IP name, the AAAA or A6 record query is passed to BIND.

When the BIG-IP GTM system receives an IPv6 AAAA or A6 record query for a wide IP name, and the wide IP pool does not have any IPv6 virtual servers available or configured, the AAAA or A6 record query is passed to BIND.

BIND responses to IPv6 AAAA or A6 queries

If the BIND configuration has an IPv6 AAAA or A6 record for the requested name, BIND sends a NOERROR response that contains the addresses specified in the BIND configuration.

If the BIND configuration does not have an IPv6 AAAA or A6 record, but does have an IPv4 A record for the requested wide IP name, BIND responds with a NOERROR response that contains no data.

Note: The requesting Local Domain Name System (LDNS) server then sends an IPv4 A record query, to which the BIG-IP GTM system processes and responds as appropriate.

If the BIND configuration does not contain any resource records for the requested name, BIND responds with an NXDOMAIN response. The LDNS server may cache the NXDOMAIN response and provide it to all clients requesting that record. As a result, the wide IP name is inaccessible to all users of the affected LDNS.

For more information, refer to K7852: LDNS server negative caching can result in NXDOMAIN response for entire domain.

For more information, refer to K7851: Configuring the BIG-IP GTM or Link Controller systems to provide NOERROR responses for IPv6 queries.
